title: camelid.invariant-lanes/v1 — BASALT NVFP4 invariant-lane matrix
description: Amendment 3 §2 (stage S2, the SHA_E engine freeze): one cell per (lane × invariant). Every cell is exactly one of enforced / na / open — HONEST CELLS ONLY: an enforced cell names the test fn and the enforcement source it is bound to; an na cell carries a nonempty structural reason (file-anchored); an open cell names an unlanded phase (P4, P2b) or the flagged P3-FINDING. tests/invariant_matrix_binding.rs is the §2.4 enforcement: it include_str!-binds every file named by enforced cells (rename/move breaks the build), asserts every named test fn appears in its file's text (a fn rename fails the meta-test), validates this document against qa/invariant_lanes.json, and trips the committed fixtures (tests/fixtures/gguf/) for the refusal cells that have one. Human-readable twin: BASALT_RECON.md §13. Ratchet rules (§2.5) ride in the data file as data.

source_head.description: parent git head the matrix was extracted against; the S2 commit that adds this file is the SHA_E engine freeze

sources.description: where the invariant is signed/stated (DECISIONS.md D17 + addenda, BASALT_CONDUCTOR.md §8, basalt_eval_protocol.md, G2 summary) — columns are EXTRACTED from sources, never invented
the lane's code home; empty ONLY for a lane that has no code home (L5: Phase 2b never scheduled)

cells.description: cells[laneId][columnId] — the meta-test asserts every (lane × column) pair is present exactly once (no empty cells).

test_file.description: repo-relative file holding the named test fn (inline #[cfg(test)] mod or tests/ integration file); must be include_str!-bound in tests/invariant_matrix_binding.rs

test_fn.description: the primary test fn; the meta-test asserts `fn <test_fn>` appears in test_file's bound text

source.description: repo-relative enforcement source file (where the refusal/check lives); include_str!-bound

companion_tests.description: additional verifiable tests backing the cell; each is name-asserted against its bound file exactly like the primary

fixture.description: committed tripping fixture filename under tests/fixtures/gguf/, OR `seam:<reason>` when a file-boundary fixture is not constructible for this cell (the S1 NaN precedent)

fixture_trip.description: REQUIRED when fixture names a file: `<repo-relative test file>::<fn>` that trips the refusal on those exact bytes (referencing an existing per-lane test is fine — no duplicate execution)

reason.description: nonempty structural reason with file anchors — why the invariant has no surface on this lane

phase.description: only phases that have not landed (P4 CUDA path, P2b native quantizer) or the orchestrator-flagged P3-FINDING may hold a cell open
